lines.english: One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਗੁ ਗੋਂਡ ਬਾਣੀ ਭਗਤਾ ਕੀ ॥ ਕਬੀਰ ਜੀ ਘਰੁ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: raag gondd baanee bhagataa kee | kabeer jee ghar 1 |
lines.english: Raag Gond, The Word Of The Devotees. Kabeer Jee, First House:
O father, if I speak, what words should I utter?
When you meet a Saint, talk to him and listen.
Meeting with an unsaintly person, just remain silent. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੈਸੇ ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮ ਰਵਿ ਰਹੀਐ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: jaise raam naam rav raheeai |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Speak such words, by which you may remain absorbed in the Name of the Lord. ||1||Pause||
To speak with a fool is to babble uselessly. ||2||
Speaking with the Saints, one becomes generous.
If I do not speak, what can the poor wretch do? ||3||
Says Kabeer, the empty pitcher makes noise,
By speaking and only speaking, corruption only increases.
Gond:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਰੂ ਮਰੈ ਨਰੁ ਕਾਮਿ ਨ ਆਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: naroo marai nar kaam na aavai |
lines.english: When a man dies, he is of no use to anyone.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਰਿਆ ਹੋਇ ਸੁ ਕਬਹੁ ਨ ਡੋਲੈ ॥੪॥੧॥
lines.translit: bhariaa hoe su kabahu na ddolai |4|1|
lines.english: but that which is full makes no sound. ||4||1||
But when an animal dies, it is used in ten ways. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੈ ਕਿਆ ਜਾਨਉ ਬਾਬਾ ਰੇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: mai kiaa jaanau baabaa re |1| rahaau |
lines.english: What do I know, O Baba? ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਾਡ ਜਲੇ ਜੈਸੇ ਲਕਰੀ ਕਾ ਤੂਲਾ ॥
lines.translit: haadd jale jaise lakaree kaa toolaa |
lines.english: His bones burn, like a bundle of logs;

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਪਨੇ ਕਰਮ ਕੀ ਗਤਿ ਮੈ ਕਿਆ ਜਾਨਉ ॥
lines.translit: apane karam kee gat mai kiaa jaanau |
lines.english: What do I know, about the state of my karma?

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹੁ ਕਬੀਰ ਤਬ ਹੀ ਨਰੁ ਜਾਗੈ ॥
lines.translit: kahu kabeer tab hee nar jaagai |
lines.english: Says Kabeer, the man wakes up,

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੇਸ ਜਲੇ ਜੈਸੇ ਘਾਸ ਕਾ ਪੂਲਾ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: kes jale jaise ghaas kaa poolaa |2|
lines.english: his hair burns like a bale of hay. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਮ ਕਾ ਡੰਡੁ ਮੂੰਡ ਮਹਿ ਲਾਗੈ ॥੩॥੨॥
lines.translit: jam kaa ddandd moondd meh laagai |3|2|
lines.english: only when the Messenger of Death hits him over the head with his club. ||3||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਕਾਸਿ ਗਗਨੁ ਪਾਤਾਲਿ ਗਗਨੁ ਹੈ ਚਹੁ ਦਿਸਿ ਗਗਨੁ ਰਹਾਇਲੇ ॥
lines.translit: aakaas gagan paataal gagan hai chahu dis gagan rahaaeile |
lines.english: The Celestial Lord is in the Akaashic ethers of the skies, the Celestial Lord is in the nether regions of the underworld; in the four directions, the Celestial Lord is pervading.
Gond:

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਨਦ ਮੂਲੁ ਸਦਾ ਪੁਰਖੋਤਮੁ ਘਟੁ ਬਿਨਸੈ ਗਗਨੁ ਨ ਜਾਇਲੇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: aanad mool sadaa purakhotam ghatt binasai gagan na jaaeile |1|
lines.english: The Supreme Lord God is forever the source of bliss. When the vessel of the body perishes, the Celestial Lord does not perish. ||1||
I have become sad,

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਹੁ ਜੀਉ ਆਇ ਕਹਾ ਗਇਓ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: eihu jeeo aae kahaa geo |1| rahaau |
lines.english: wondering where the soul comes from, and where it goes. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੰਚ ਤਤੁ ਮਿਲਿ ਕਾਇਆ ਕੀਨੑੀ ਤਤੁ ਕਹਾ ਤੇ ਕੀਨੁ ਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: panch tat mil kaaeaa keenaee tat kahaa te keen re |
lines.english: The body is formed from the union of the five tatvas; but where were the five tatvas created?

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਰਮ ਬਧ ਤੁਮ ਜੀਉ ਕਹਤ ਹੌ ਕਰਮਹਿ ਕਿਨਿ ਜੀਉ ਦੀਨੁ ਰੇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: karam badh tum jeeo kehat hau karameh kin jeeo deen re |2|
lines.english: You say that the soul is tied to its karma, but who gave karma to the body? ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਮਹਿ ਤਨੁ ਹੈ ਤਨ ਮਹਿ ਹਰਿ ਹੈ ਸਰਬ ਨਿਰੰਤਰਿ ਸੋਇ ਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: har meh tan hai tan meh har hai sarab nirantar soe re |
lines.english: The body is contained in the Lord, and the Lord is contained in the body. He is permeating within all.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਿ ਕਬੀਰ ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮੁ ਨ ਛੋਡਉ ਸਹਜੇ ਹੋਇ ਸੁ ਹੋਇ ਰੇ ॥੩॥੩॥
lines.translit: keh kabeer raam naam na chhoddau sahaje hoe su hoe re |3|3|
lines.english: Says Kabeer, I shall not renounce the Lord's Name. I shall accept whatever happens. ||3||3||
One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਗੁ ਗੋਂਡ ਬਾਣੀ ਕਬੀਰ ਜੀਉ ਕੀ ਘਰੁ ੨ ॥
lines.translit: raag gondd baanee kabeer jeeo kee ghar 2 |
lines.english: Raag Gond, The Word Of Kabeer Jee, Second House:
They tied my arms, bundled me up, and threw me before an elephant.
But the elephant ran away, trumpeting,
The elephant driver struck him on the head, and infuriated him.
"I am a sacrifice to this image of the Lord." ||1||
The Qazi shouted at the driver to drive the elephant on. ||1||Pause||
O my Lord and Master, You are my strength.
But the elephant did not move; instead, he began to meditate.
He yelled out, "O driver, I shall cut you into pieces.
Hit him, and drive him on!"
that you have made him into a bundle and thrown him before the elephant?
What sin has this Saint committed,
The Lord God abides within his mind. ||2||
Lifting up the bundle, the elephant bows down before it.
Three times, he tried to do it.
The Qazi could not understand it; he was blind. ||3||
